title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure poses a significant risk of data theft, service disruption, and financial loss. By exploiting compromised CI/CD pipelines, attackers can deploy malicious code at scale, potentially affecting multiple cloud-hosted applications and services.
recommended_actions: Immediately review and secure CI/CD pipelines by implementing multi-factor authentication (MFA), role-based access controls, and regular security audits.
Deploy endpoint detection and response (EDR) solutions to monitor and mitigate suspicious activities in cloud environments.
Ensure regular backups of critical data are stored securely offline and test recovery processes to minimize downtime in case of an attack.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity.

title: A flaw has been found in GL.iNet GL-MT3000 4.4.5. This impacts the function snprintf of the file ...
description: A flaw has been found in GL.iNet GL-MT3000 4.4.5. This impacts the function snprintf of the file /cgi-bin/glc of the component FTP Protocol Handler. Executing a manipulation of the argument media_dir can lead to command injection. It is possible to launch the attack remotely. Upgrading to version 4.8.1 will fix this issue. You should upgrade the affected component. The vendor explains: "In version 4.8.1, before writing media_dir to the FTP configuration command, the code escapes single quotes us

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary commands via command injection by manipulating the 'media_dir' argument in the FTP Protocol Handler. This could lead to unauthorized access, data exfiltration, or system compromise.
recommended_actions: Immediately upgrade to GL.iNet GL-MT3000 firmware version 4.8.1 or later to patch the vulnerability.
Restrict access to the affected FTP service by implementing network-level access controls (e.g., firewall rules).
Monitor network traffic for unusual activity and implement intrusion detection/prevention systems to detect potential exploitation attempts.

title: clash-verge-service-ipc before 2.3.0 has a world-reachable IPC endpoint, leading to local...
description: clash-verge-service-ipc before 2.3.0 has a world-reachable IPC endpoint, leading to local privilege escalation.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in clash-verge-service-ipc before version 2.3.0 allows unauthorized local users to exploit a world-reachable IPC endpoint, potentially leading to privilege escalation. This could enable attackers to execute arbitrary commands or gain elevated permissions on the affected system.
recommended_actions: Immediately upgrade to clash-verge-service-ipc version 2.3.0 or later to mitigate the vulnerability.
Restrict access to the IPC endpoint by implementing proper access controls and network segmentation.
Conduct a security audit to identify any unauthorized access or exploitation attempts and monitor systems for unusual activity.

title: A vulnerability was identified in theonedev onedev up to 15.0.5. This vulnerability affects the...
description: A vulnerability was identified in theonedev onedev up to 15.0.5. This vulnerability affects the function canAccessIssue of the file /issues/ of the component Pull Request Handler. Such manipulation of the argument issue leads to improper authorization. It is possible to launch the attack remotely. Upgrading to version 15.0.6 is able to resolve this issue. It is advisable to upgrade the affected component.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in the Pull Request Handler allows improper authorization, potentially enabling unauthorized access to sensitive issue data. This could lead to data leakage or unauthorized modifications to issue tracking systems.
recommended_actions: Upgrade theonedev to version 15.0.6 immediately to patch the vulnerability.
Audit access logs to identify any unauthorized access or suspicious activity related to the /issues/ endpoint.
Implement additional security measures, such as stricter access controls, to mitigate risks of similar vulnerabilities in the future.

title: A security vulnerability has been detected in Jinher OA 1.0. This affects an unknown function of...
description: A security vulnerability has been detected in Jinher OA 1.0. This affects an unknown function of the file nextselectplan.aspx. Such manipulation of the argument httpOID leads to sql injection. The attack may be launched remotely. The exploit has been disclosed publicly and may be used. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows remote attackers to perform SQL injection via the httpOID parameter in the nextselectplan.aspx file, potentially leading to unauthorized access, data leakage, or database manipulation.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply input validation and sanitization for the httpOID parameter to prevent SQL injection attacks.
Deploy web application firewalls (WAF) to detect and block malicious SQL injection attempts.
Contact the vendor again and escalate the issue to ensure they address the vulnerability or provide a patch.

title: A flaw has been found in perfree go-fastdfs-web up to 1.3.7. Affected is the function checkServer...
description: A flaw has been found in perfree go-fastdfs-web up to 1.3.7. Affected is the function checkServer of the file /install/checkServer of the component Installation Endpoint. Executing a manipulation can lead to server-side request forgery. The attack can be executed remotely. The exploit has been published and may be used. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in the checkServer function of the go-fastdfs-web installation endpoint allows for server-side request forgery (SSRF). This could enable attackers to manipulate server requests, potentially leading to unauthorized access to internal systems, data exfiltration, or further exploitation of internal services.
recommended_actions: Immediately upgrade to a patched or newer version of go-fastdfs-web if available. If no patch is available, consider disabling or restricting access to the vulnerable installation endpoint (/install/checkServer).
Implement strict firewall rules and network segmentation to limit access to internal systems and prevent unauthorized requests from being executed.
Monitor logs for unusual or suspicious activity targeting the /install/checkServer endpoint and implement intrusion detection mechanisms to identify potential exploitation attempts.

title: A security flaw has been discovered in iAI Lab PDF AI App 4.21.0 on Android. Impacted is the...
description: A security flaw has been discovered in iAI Lab PDF AI App 4.21.0 on Android. Impacted is the function getExternalCacheDir of the component chatpdf.pro. Performing a manipulation of the argument _display_name results in path traversal. The attack requires a local approach. The exploit has been released to the public and may be used for attacks. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows for path traversal attacks through the manipulation of the _display_name argument in the getExternalCacheDir function. This could potentially lead to unauthorized access to sensitive files and data stored on the device. However, the attack requires local access, limiting its scope.
recommended_actions: Advise users to update to a patched version of the app as soon as it becomes available or consider using alternative applications until a fix is released.
Implement mobile device management (MDM) policies to restrict the installation or use of vulnerable applications within the organization.
Educate users about the risks of installing untrusted applications or granting unnecessary permissions to apps.

title:
Protocol::HTTP2 versions through 1.12 for Perl is vulnerable to a HTTP/2 Bomb.

Protocol::HTTP2's...
description:
Protocol::HTTP2 versions through 1.12 for Perl is vulnerable to a HTTP/2 Bomb.

Protocol::HTTP2's inbound HPACK path has no header-list size limit, so a small HTTP/2 request can expand into large server memory (the "HTTP/2 bomb").

The headers_decode method materialises a full key+value copy per indexed reference with no running size check, and the stream_header_block_add method appends (since version 1.12) every CONTINUATION frame to the per-stream buffer unbounded.

MAX_HEADER_LIST_SIZE (defau

title: CVE-2026-10167 - A weakness has been identified in OUSL-GROUP-BrinaryBrains School Student Management System up to 1e
description: A weakness has been identified in OUSL-GROUP-BrinaryBrains School Student Management System up to 1e70e5ad1125b86dca4ee086eb6bb121f17708b6. This impacts the function sign_auth_cookie of the file application/controllers/Login.php of the component MY_Controller. Executing a manipulation of the argument role can lead to improper authentication. It is possible to launch the attack remotely. The exploit has been made available to the public and could be used for attacks. This product takes the approach of rolling releases to provide continious delivery. Therefore, version details for affected and updated releases are not available. The project was informed of the problem early through an issue report but has not responded yet.

title: CVE-2026-8382 - The Advanced Custom Fields (ACF®) plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to authorization bypass in all
description: The Advanced Custom Fields (ACF®) plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to authorization bypass in all versions up to, and including, 6.8.1. This is due to the plugin not properly verifying that a user is authorized to perform an action. This makes it possible for unauthenticated attackers to overwrite the post_title and post_content of any post bound to a publicly accessible acf_form() instance by injecting values into the _post_title and _post_content parameters of a form submission request.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-8382.
